frozen_components: open-source large language models (frozen at inference time)
architecture.summary: The system uses open-source large language models with targeted prompts to extract structured biographical information (age, occupation, origin) from unstructured newspaper obituary text. Information flows from raw text through prompt-driven prompting strategies into structured extracted fields. The paper treats prompts as configurable "promptbooks" (an analogue to traditional codebooks in qualitative research) and evaluates how different model sizes and prompting techniques affect extraction accuracy and error patterns.

biblio.title: From Codebooks to Promptbooks: Extracting Information from Text with Generative Large Language Models

claims.statement: GenAI allows researchers to define their own IE tasks and execute them via targeted prompts, changing IE from requiring specialized models to using flexible, accessible prompt-driven approaches.
Sociologists can develop and evaluate prompt architectures for information extraction tasks by turning codebooks into 'promptbooks.'
Different model sizes and prompting techniques affect extraction performance.
Non-random error patterns in extraction can bias downstream analyses, presenting a risk when using GenAI for information extraction.
